title: Config Honour Model Evaluation Loop
goal: Run dynamic model CLI evaluation and optimisation loops across Copilot/Claude Haiku, Gemini 3 Flash, and Codex Spark to make repo and local configuration effects visible, well-defined, and followed by agents.
non_goals: Do not convert this into a formal benchmark; keep it an adaptive optimisation loop.
Do not hard-code one vendor model's quirks as universal workflow unless repeated evidence supports it.
Do not expose private local-only configuration as shared repo authority.

intent.outcome: Improve package/harness behavior so agents notice and respect `.agentic-workspace/config.toml` and `.agentic-workspace/config.local.toml` settings during realistic work.
intent.constraints: Keep changes bounded to model CLI harness scenarios/scoring, config-facing command/report guidance, docs/contracts, and focused tests.
intent.latitude: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: Archive this lane and close implemented issues after final closeout trust check, commit, and push.
execution.proof: Executed real harness runs across Copilot/Claude Haiku, Gemini 3 Flash, and Codex Spark; fixed repeated ambiguity; reran focused tests and make check.

invariants: Configuration fields must have inspectable effects in CLI/report/implementer-context surfaces.
Repo config is shared authority; local config is local-advisory and must not become shared repo policy.
Quality fit outranks token saving; token saving is useful only when configuration and proof remain safe.
Model CLI prompts should stay realistic and should not explicitly tell agents to use Agentic Workspace.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Make Agentic Workspace configuration a reliable, visible operating substrate for agents across tools and capability levels.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Config is now visible through compact CLI output, seeded during init, referenced at point of edit, and exercised by dynamic model CLI probes; host-repo subsystem ownership can now route path-specific proof hints.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Mixed-agent workflow evaluation remains out of scope for this lane.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Mechanical scoring is useful only as a guardrail; the optimisation loop should emphasize adaptive scenarios, runner judgment, and model self-explanation to expose new gaps.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: none for this slice after final closeout trust check
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: future optimisation loops can add more dynamic scenarios and mixed-agent workflows when requested

intent_interpretation.literal request: Run extensive evaluation+optimisation loops on all three target models with the goal of making sure that agents honour settings in config.toml and config.local.toml.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Use real model behavior to find where configuration has weak or invisible workflow effects, then tighten product/harness guidance and rerun.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Add or refine config-focused model CLI scenarios, run Copilot/Haiku, Gemini 3 Flash, and Codex Spark, implement product or harness fixes for repeated ambiguity, and close with evidence.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Check whether changes make configuration effects more visible and actionable without making local config shared authority.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: tools/model-cli-harness/**; scripts/model_cli_harness/**; docs/maintainer/model-cli-dogfooding-harness.md; .agentic-workspace/docs/*config*.md; .agentic-workspace/docs/delegation-posture-contract.md; src/agentic_workspace/cli.py; tests/**; this execplan/state for closeout
execution_bounds.required validation commands: targeted model CLI harness tests; selected real harness executions for copilot, gemini, and codex; make check before commit.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated package modules, unrelated docs hierarchy cleanup, external provider credentials, or shared config values not needed by this lane.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, config command output, config docs, model CLI harness docs/scripts/suite/fixtures, run outputs, and narrow CLI/report surfaces that expose config effects.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Run config-focused model CLI probes, tighten repeated ambiguity, rerun targeted probes, then prove with harness tests and make check.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Config-honouring model evaluation and optimisation loop.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Use real model evidence; keep local config local-advisory; do not take control away from humans unless config says auto; quality fit outranks token saving.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: A better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.

invariants: Config effects must be queryable from compact CLI/report surfaces.
Local config may influence local runtime posture but must remain non-authoritative for shared repo state.
Model evaluations should stress realistic human prompts without explicit Agentic Workspace steering.
Fix repeated product ambiguity immediately; route one-off provider/model weakness as evidence only unless high consequence.
validation_commands: selected real harness runs for copilot, gemini, and codex adapters
completion_criteria: Config-focused scenarios or scoring exist for shared config and local config behavior.
All three target adapters have real evaluation evidence unless a provider/runtime blocks execution.
Repeated ambiguity is fixed in product/harness/docs/tests.
Reruns show improvement or remaining risk is explicitly routed.

execution_run.what happened: Ingested #719-#728, added compact config output and init seeding, made config references visible at edit points, added managed-state warnings, added ownership diagnostics and optional subsystem ownership, tightened model CLI harness scenarios/scoring, fixed generated TypeScript stdout truncation, and reran dynamic model probes.
execution_run.scope touched: workspace CLI/config/report/proof/ownership surfaces, generated command packages, planning installer/checks, model CLI harness fixtures/scenarios/scorer, schema docs, and closeout planning state.
execution_run.changed surfaces: src/agentic_workspace/cli.py; contracts and generated CLI packages; .agentic-workspace config/ownership/docs/state; model CLI harness scripts/fixtures/suites/docs; planning installer/check tests.
execution_run.validations run: Focused subsystem tests passed; contract tooling passed; structured file inventory passed; schema reference annotation check passed; generated command package checks passed including Docker conformance; package planning tests/lint passed; make check passed.
execution_run.result for continuation: no required continuation for this slice
execution_run.next step: Archive this execplan, commit, push, and close #719-#728 except unrelated older backlog.

finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: reduced; dynamic evaluation guidance now emphasizes judged behavior over mechanical benchmark scoring

proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: make check passed; targeted tests for workspace CLI, model CLI harness, contract tooling, structured file inventory, schema references, generated command packages, and planning package passed; real Copilot, Codex Spark, and Gemini runs were reviewed with provider/runtime instability separated from product findings.

proof_execution_evidence.evidence_ref: full maintainer check passed before closeout

intent_satisfaction.original intent: user request: extensive eval+optimisation loops for config.toml and config.local.toml
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: The lane ran dynamic optimisation loops across Copilot/Haiku, Codex Spark, and Gemini; repeated config visibility and closeout scoring issues were fixed; new related issues #719-#728 were ingested and implemented.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: Config-aware workflow and model CLI evaluation surfaces are stronger: compact config output, init config seeding, point-of-edit references, ownership diagnostics, subsystem proof hints, harness dynamic scenarios, provider/runtime classification, and generated TypeScript stdout safety.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (Memory/Docs/Config): workspace config docs, ownership authority docs, generated schema reference, and repo/payload ownership ledgers

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The user requested config-honouring optimisation loops and new related issues between loops; all issues created for this slice were implemented, dynamic model evidence was reviewed, provider/runtime noise was separated, and full proof passed.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: This execplan, generated schema reference, ownership subsystem contract, model CLI harness scenarios, and GitHub issue closeout comments.
closure_check.reopen trigger: Future real model runs show agents still cannot discover or honor config/local config settings despite compact config and subsystem projections.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: Weak models need compact config output rather than full raw config reports.
Closeout scoring must avoid treating provider/runtime failures as product regressions.
Ownership ledgers need host-repo subsystem hints for path-scoped proof and escalation.
Generated TypeScript CLI adapter can truncate stdout if it writes asynchronously and exits immediately.
Optimisation loops should privilege dynamic scenario judgment over deterministic metric scoring.
signals dismissed: Gemini provider capacity and AttachConsole stderr were classified as environment/provider instability, not product findings.

discard.summary: Raw model run transcripts and provider noise remain harness evidence, not durable docs.

config_check.summary: Compact config output and config edit references now expose shared/local configuration effects to weak model tools.

docs.summary: Ownership docs now describe optional [[subsystems]] boundaries and proof hints.

drift_log: 2026-05-04: Ingested and implemented #719-#728 while running dynamic config-honouring model optimisation loops.
2026-05-04: User clarified that deterministic metrics are not the main optimisation loop; retained scorer guardrails but treated judged dynamic behavior as primary evidence.
